title: Marsdenia lanata
taxonomy: Marsdenia lanata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This family of plants is commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. Marsdenia lanata is further classified as being part of the genus Marsdenia, which consists of around 100 species of plants. The genus Marsdenia is native to Asia and Australia, with some species found in other parts of the world. Marsdenia lanata is a type of twining vine that is native to Australia.

intro: Marsdenia lanata, also known as the hairy milk vine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Marsdenia lanata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Marsdenia lanata is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of extracts and tinctures to treat various health conditions.
